grammarMapping.promotionGate: A movement candidate must not promote from object-track or pressure fit alone; persona routeability and collision-loss share must preserve or improve.

summary.promotionRule: A movement candidate must not promote from object-track or pressure fit alone; persona routeability and collision-loss share must preserve or improve.
summary.releaseRead: Guardians now has 6 grammar-backed movement candidate rows. The first priority is Stage-five stress routeability relief because routeability is 5.4/10 overall and 4.5/10 in the weakest stage-five stress group.

candidates.title: Stage-five stress routeability relief
candidates.problem: The weakest player-facing movement problem is not missing pressure; it is that stage-five stress is collision-dominated and does not convert into clean-clear routeability.
candidates.proposedCandidate: Create a bounded stage-five movement candidate that slightly widens lower-field escape/read lanes and preserves shot pressure while reducing collision-loss share.
candidates.playerMeaning: A strong player should feel pressure and still see a route, rather than feeling that lower-field movement simply crowds them out.
candidates.promotionGate: Accept only if midrun-stage-five-stress routeability improves and object-track/pressure scores do not materially regress.
candidates.nextMeasurement: Browser-backed persona before/after run at stage five with score, survival, collision-loss share, and object-track summary.

grammarPrimitive.meaning: A time and position window where a skilled player can deliberately score, rescue, survive, or earn a perfect route.

candidates.title: Scout dive duration and shape fit
candidates.problem: Runtime dives are much shorter than the reference proxy median (2.65s vs 5.5s), even though x/y span and event coverage are strong.
candidates.proposedCandidate: Try a slower, longer scout-dive profile in a controlled candidate while preserving current first-dive timing and stage-one readability.
candidates.playerMeaning: Dives should read as graceful arcade pressure with time to aim, not as abrupt short hops.
candidates.promotionGate: Accept only if duration fit improves and routeability does not fall below the current baseline.
candidates.nextMeasurement: Runtime-vs-reference movement comparison plus routeability review for baseline and candidate profiles.

grammarPrimitive.meaning: A single enemy leaves formation, descends, attacks, and exits or returns.

candidates.title: Flagship escort readable pressure
candidates.problem: Guardians has reference-backed escort pressure evidence, but the candidate loop has not yet isolated whether escort-linked dives are readable scoring opportunities or only extra density.
candidates.proposedCandidate: Create a named escort-linked-dive candidate with explicit escort spacing/lag variants and before/after routeability deltas.
candidates.playerMeaning: The flagship should feel like a special coordinated threat, not just another object in the lower-field crowd.
candidates.promotionGate: Accept only if escort grouping remains visible and persona routeability is preserved or improved.
candidates.nextMeasurement: Contact-sheet and runtime tracklet before/after focused on linked flagship/escort path overlap.

grammarPrimitive.meaning: A leader or boss moves with escorts or paired enemies in a coordinated attack.

candidates.title: Bottom wrap and return readability
candidates.problem: Runtime wrap coverage exists, but the next quality question is whether wrap/return behavior is visually readable and fair when pressure increases.
candidates.proposedCandidate: Instrument wrap/return path variants with explicit lower-field routeability and visual-overlap checks before changing runtime constants.
candidates.playerMeaning: The player should understand where a returning enemy will go and why a collision happened.
candidates.promotionGate: Accept only if wrap count/coverage remains healthy and collision-loss share does not rise.
candidates.nextMeasurement: Before/after visual sheet with wrap path overlays and persona collision-loss deltas.

grammarPrimitive.meaning: Enemies pass below the player field or screen boundary and re-enter or resolve their path.

candidates.title: Rack drift and opening cadence
candidates.problem: Opening/rack motion is useful enough to guard, but future theming and variant work could accidentally break the stable rack read.
candidates.proposedCandidate: Keep rack drift as a guardrail row and require future visual/theme variants to preserve rack stability and opening cadence before promotion.
candidates.playerMeaning: The first screen should read like a coherent arcade formation before individual threats peel away.
candidates.promotionGate: Accept only if rack stability, opening cadence, and visual-role separation remain above current floors.
candidates.nextMeasurement: Run frame-motion/object-track checks after any theme or sprite-scale change.

grammarPrimitive.meaning: A formed group shifts, pulses, or marches while maintaining a recognizable rack.

candidates.title: Movement-linked audio cue hooks
candidates.problem: Movement candidates currently track visual/routeability risk better than audio meaning; dive and escort changes should also say when audio needs to warn, reward, or clarify.
candidates.proposedCandidate: Add audio cue-hook fields to movement candidate reports before tuning dive/escort/wrap behavior further.
candidates.playerMeaning: Sound should help the player understand threat and reward timing rather than simply decorating the scene.
candidates.promotionGate: Accept only if movement candidates declare whether audio hooks are unchanged, required, or intentionally deferred.
candidates.nextMeasurement: Add cue-hook fields to the next before/after candidate artifact and rerun audio-scene review where hooks change.

grammarPrimitive.meaning: A single enemy leaves formation, descends, attacks, and exits or returns.

nextSteps: Build a browser-backed before/after routeability capture for the top candidate.
Keep all movement candidates linked to primitive ids and axes before runtime promotion.
Only tune runtime constants after routeability, collision-safety, shot-window, and visual-readability gates are present.
